title: Maritime distress incidents increase near Strait of Hormuz amid US blockade enforcement
summary: Maritime monitoring accounts report elevated distress incidents and disrupted shipping patterns near the Strait of Hormuz as the US enforces its blockade of Iranian ports. Tanker charter rates remain volatile.
contents:
# Maritime Disruption Intensifies Near Strait of Hormuz

## Situation
The US-imposed blockade of the Strait of Hormuz continues generating significant maritime disruption. Shipping patterns through the world's most critical oil chokepoint face sustained interference, affecting commercial vessel operations and raising safety concerns.

## Reported Effects
- Disrupted shipping lanes increase collision and grounding risks
- Tanker charter rates remain highly volatile
- Iranian President Pezeshkian condemned the US "siege" of Iranian ports as "intolerable"
- Iran warned the only place for the US in the Gulf lies "at the bottom of its waters"

## Environmental and Water Security
The prolonged military operations and blockade affect the Persian Gulf's maritime environment. Port operations disruptions cascade through regional water supply and desalination infrastructure dependent on stable shipping access.

## SAR Monitoring
Maritime distress monitoring accounts track increased ALERFA and INCERFA phase situations in the region. No major DETRESFA declarations reported in the immediate time period.

## Tags
strait-of-hormuz, blockade, maritime-distress, iran, shipping, persian-gulf, water-security
